lede: A look at June 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jean Moulin and Jean-Marie Le Pen, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Wikimedia Foundation
events.desc: The Wikimedia Foundation is founded in St. Petersburg, Florida.

events.title: Imam Reza shrine bombing
events.desc: The 1994 Imam Reza shrine bomb explosion in Iran leaves at least 25 dead and 70 to 300 injured.

events.title: International Conference on the Holocaust and Genocide
events.desc: The International Conference on the Holocaust and Genocide opens in Tel Aviv, despite attempts by the Turkish government to cancel it, as it included presentations on the Armenian genocide.

events.title: Mali Federation
events.desc: The Mali Federation gains independence from France (it later splits into Mali and Senegal).

events.title: Venezuela
events.desc: A Venezuelan Super-Constellation crashes in the Atlantic Ocean off Asbury Park, New Jersey, killing 74 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Royal Air Force launches Operation Bellicose, the first shuttle bombing raid of the war. Avro Lancaster bombers damage the V-2 rocket production facilities at the Zeppelin Works while en route to an air base in Algeria.

events.title: 28th International Eucharistic Congress
events.desc: The 28th International Eucharistic Congress begins in Chicago, with over 250,000 spectators attending the opening procession.

events.title: Buckingham and Carnatic Mills
events.desc: Workers of Buckingham and Carnatic Mills in the city of Chennai, India, begin a four-month strike.

events.title: Boxer Rebellion
events.desc: Boxer Rebellion: The Imperial Chinese Army begins a 55-day siege of the Legation Quarter in Beijing, China.

events.title: Congress of the Confederation
events.desc: The U.S. Congress adopts the Great Seal of the United States.

births.name: Xanana Gusmão
births.line: Timorese soldier and politician, 1st President of East Timor

births.name: Lionel Richie
births.line: American singer, songwriter, pianist, producer, and actor

deaths.text: Mark Robson (film director), Canadian-American director and producer
Georges Lemaître, Belgian priest, physicist, and astronomer
Bruno Frank, German author, poet, and playwright
Abraham Gotthelf Kästner, German mathematician and academic
